sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, William, presents with a history of injuring his right knee at a trampoline park a week ago, describing a "snap" and immediate pain.
He reports hearing a pop when he landed and experiencing pain on the outside of the knee, with more pain on the lateral aspect.
He has tried taking Tylenol, which provided minimal relief.
He reports elevating the knee and using it for a week, but has not used ice or heat.
He denies any previous similar injuries or conditions.
He has a history of hypertension, for which he is taking 20mg of lisinopril, but admits to not consistently taking his medication and not measuring his blood pressure regularly.
He also has a history of diabetes, for which he is taking metformin 500mg, but reports inconsistent monitoring of his blood sugar levels.
Review of Systems: The patient reports no other symptoms.
Current Medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily, metformin 500mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 180/80.
Physical exam: The patient's heart rate and rhythm are normal, with no murmurs.
Lung sounds are clear bilaterally.
The knee exam reveals ecchymosis and edema on the lateral aspect, with a slight limp and pain on the outside of the knee, but no pain on the inside.
Range of motion is intact, but painful with movement.
Test Results: Right knee x-ray shows no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient presents with a lateral ligament strain of the right knee, hypertension, and diabetes.
Problem list: Lateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension; Diabetes; Hypertension; Diabetes.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling related to the lateral ligament strain.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the area and prevent further injury.
I continued the patient's lisinopril 20mg daily and ordered an echo to assess the blood pressure management.
I also ordered an A1c and lipid panel to monitor the patient's diabetes management.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in two weeks to review the results of the echo, A1c, and lipid panel, and to reassess the patient's progress with the physical therapy.
